q: For a chemical reaction, the enthalpy change is independent of
question: For a chemical reaction, the enthalpy change is independent of
options: the physical states of the reactants and products.
the chemical nature of the reactants and products.
the various intermediate stages through which the reaction occurs.
the enthalpy values of the system at the beginning and at the end.

Identify the reactions in which, at constant pressure, work is done by the surroundings on the system:

III.    C (s, graphite) + H2O (g) → CO (g) + H2 (g)

IV.    H2O (s) → H2O (l)

EA(Cl) = 365 and ΔHf(LiCl) = -400 (all in kJ mole-1)

q:
C (s) + O2 (g) → CO2 (g);   ΔH = -94.3 kcal/mol

O2 (g) → 2O (g);   ΔH = 117.4 kcal/mol

CO (g) → C (g) + O (g);   ΔH = 230.6 kcal/mol

Find the value of ΔH for C (s) → C (g) in kcal/mol.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The chemical reactions of gold were an important area of study for alchemists. Consider the reactions written below,

$$ \begin{array}{ll} \mathrm{Au}(\mathrm{OH})_3+4 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HAuCl}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, & \Delta \mathrm{H}=-28 \mathrm{kcal} \\ \mathrm{Au}(\mathrm{OH})_3+4 \mathrm{HBr} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HAuBr}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, & \Delta \mathrm{H}=-36.8 \mathrm{kcal} \end{array} $$

In a given experiment, 0.44 kcal of heat was absorbed when one mole of $\mathrm{HAuBr}_4$ was mixed with 4 moles of HCl . Find the fraction of $\mathrm{HAuBr}_4$ that changed into $\mathrm{HAuCl}_4$ (percentage conversion)
question:
The chemical reactions of gold were an important area of study for alchemists. Consider the reactions written below,

$$ \begin{array}{ll} \mathrm{Au}(\mathrm{OH})_3+4 \mathrm{HCl} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HAuCl}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, & \Delta \mathrm{H}=-28 \mathrm{kcal} \\ \mathrm{Au}(\mathrm{OH})_3+4 \mathrm{HBr} \longrightarrow \mathrm{HAuBr}_4+3 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}, & \Delta \mathrm{H}=-36.8 \mathrm{kcal} \end{array} $$

In a given experiment, 0.44 kcal of heat was absorbed when one mole of $\mathrm{HAuBr}_4$ was mixed with 4 moles of HCl . Find the fraction of $\mathrm{HAuBr}_4$ that changed into $\mathrm{HAuCl}_4$ (percentage conversion)

subject: Chemistry
q: For a 1 M solution of a weak acid, the enthalpy of neutralisation with a strong base is $-56.1 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. If the ionization enthalpy of the acid is $1.5 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and the enthalpy of neutralization of a strong acid by a strong base is -57.3 kJ equiv ${ }^{-1}$, find the $\%$ ionization of the weak acid in the molar solution (assume that the acid is monobasic).
question: For a 1 M solution of a weak acid, the enthalpy of neutralisation with a strong base is $-56.1 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$. If the ionization enthalpy of the acid is $1.5 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mol}^{-1}$ and the enthalpy of neutralization of a strong acid by a strong base is -57.3 kJ equiv ${ }^{-1}$, find the $\%$ ionization of the weak acid in the molar solution (assume that the acid is monobasic).

subject: Chemistry
q:
Using the data provided, find the resonance energy of isoprene $\left(\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_8\right)$.

It is given that $\Delta \mathrm{HC}_{\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}}=615 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{C}}=348 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mole}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}}=413 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{H}}=435 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1}$ The standard heat of sublimation of graphite is $718 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1}$ and the heat of formation of $\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_8(\mathrm{~g})$ is 79 $\mathrm{kJmole}^{-1}$

(Write your answer in kcal mole-1; nearest integer).
question:
Using the data provided, find the resonance energy of isoprene $\left(\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_8\right)$.

It is given that $\Delta \mathrm{HC}_{\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}}=615 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{O}-\mathrm{C}}=348 \mathrm{~kJ} \mathrm{~mole}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{C}-\mathrm{H}}=413 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1} ; \Delta \mathrm{H}_{\mathrm{H}-\mathrm{H}}=435 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1}$ The standard heat of sublimation of graphite is $718 \mathrm{kJmole}^{-1}$ and the heat of formation of $\mathrm{C}_5 \mathrm{H}_8(\mathrm{~g})$ is 79 $\mathrm{kJmole}^{-1}$

(Write your answer in kcal mole-1; nearest integer).

subject: Chemistry
q: On complete combustion of 1 g of starch, $\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{10} \mathrm{O}_5\right)_x$, to form $\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$, 4.6 kcal of heat is released. The heats of formation of $\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$ are -94.2 and $-68.4 \mathrm{kcal} / \mathrm{mol}$, respectively. Find the magnitude of the standard enthalpy of formation of 1 g of starch (in kcal).
question: On complete combustion of 1 g of starch, $\left(\mathrm{C}_6 \mathrm{H}_{10} \mathrm{O}_5\right)_x$, to form $\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$, 4.6 kcal of heat is released. The heats of formation of $\mathrm{CO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ and $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(\mathrm{l})$ are -94.2 and $-68.4 \mathrm{kcal} / \mathrm{mol}$, respectively. Find the magnitude of the standard enthalpy of formation of 1 g of starch (in kcal).
